audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Samaritan woman, living water, worship in Spirit/truth, I am saying, harvest, Samaritan belief, and royal official's son.

verses.restored: When therefore Jesus knew that the Pharisees heard Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John,
though Jesus himself was not baptizing, but his disciples,
he left Judea and went away again into Galilee.
It was necessary for him to pass through Samaria.
He comes therefore to a city of Samaria called Sychar, near the field Jacob had given to Joseph his son.
Jacob's well was there. Jesus, wearied from the journey, was sitting thus by the well. It was about the sixth hour.
A woman from Samaria comes to draw water. Jesus says to her, Give me to drink.
For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.
The Samaritan woman says to him, How is it that you, being a Judean, ask a drink from me, being a Samaritan woman? For Judeans do not share vessels with Samaritans.
Jesus answered and said to her, If you knew the gift of the Divine and who it is saying to you, Give me to drink, you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.
She says to him, Lord, you have no bucket, and the well is deep; from where then do you have the living water?
Are you greater than our father Jacob, who gave us the well and drank from it himself, and his sons and his livestock?
Jesus answered and said to her, Everyone drinking from this water will thirst again.
But whoever drinks from the water that I will give will certainly not thirst into the age; but the water that I will give will become in that one a spring of water leaping up into age-long life.
The woman says to him, Lord, give me this water, so that I may not thirst nor come here to draw.
He says to her, Go, call your husband and come here.
The woman answered and said to him, I do not have a husband. Jesus says to her, You spoke beautifully/well: I do not have a husband.
For you have had five husbands, and the one whom you now have is not your husband; this you have spoken truly.
The woman says to him, Lord, I behold that you are a prophet.
Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, and you say that in Jerusalem is the place where it is necessary to worship.
Jesus says to her, Trust me, woman, an hour is coming when neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem will you worship the Father.
You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, because salvation is from the Judeans.
But an hour is coming, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in Spirit and truth; for indeed the Father seeks such ones worshiping him.
The Divine is Spirit, and those worshiping must worship in Spirit and truth.
The woman says to him, I know that Messiah is coming, the one called Anointed; when that one comes, he will announce all things to us.
Jesus says to her, I am, the one speaking to you.
At this his disciples came and were marveling that he was speaking with a woman; yet no one said, What are you seeking? or, Why are you speaking with her?
The woman therefore left her water jar, went away into the city, and says to the people,
Come, see a human who told me all things I did; can this be the Messiah?
They went out from the city and were coming to him.
Meanwhile the disciples were asking him, saying, Rabbi, eat.
But he said to them, I have food to eat that you do not know.
Therefore the disciples were saying to one another, Did someone bring him something to eat?
Jesus says to them, My food is that I may do the will of the one who sent me and complete his work.
Do you not say, There are still four months, and then comes the harvest? Look, I say to you, lift up your eyes and behold the fields, that they are white for harvest already.
The one reaping receives wages and gathers fruit into age-long life, so that the one sowing and the one reaping may rejoice together.
For in this the word is true: One is sowing and another reaping.
I sent you to reap what you have not labored for; others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.
From that city many of the Samaritans trusted into him because of the woman's word bearing witness: He told me all things I did.
When therefore the Samaritans came to him, they were asking him to remain with them; and he remained there two days.
Many more trusted because of his word.
They were saying to the woman, No longer because of your speech do we trust, for we ourselves have heard and know that this one is truly the Savior of the world.
After the two days, he went out from there into Galilee.
For Jesus himself bore witness that a prophet has no honor in his own homeland.
When therefore he came into Galilee, the Galileans received him, having seen all things he did in Jerusalem at the feast, for they also went to the feast.
He came again to Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. There was a certain royal official whose son was sick in Capernaum.
This one, hearing that Jesus had come from Judea into Galilee, went to him and asked that he come down and heal his son, for he was about to die.
Jesus therefore said to him, Unless you see signs and wonders, you will certainly not trust.
The royal official says to him, Lord, come down before my child dies.
Jesus says to him, Go; your son lives. The person trusted the word Jesus said to him and went.
Already as he was going down, his servants met him, saying that his child lives.
He inquired from them the hour in which he became better. They said to him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.
The father knew that it was at that hour in which Jesus said to him, Your son lives; and he himself trusted, and his whole house.
This again, a second sign, Jesus did after coming from Judea into Galilee.
